game_name: Joe Montana II: Sports Talk Football

danger_signals.visual_form: opposing team defensive line positioning and animation speed changes
danger_signals.meaning: increased likelihood of quarterback sack or pass incompletion

danger_signals.visual_form: player sprite slowdown or ground contact animation
danger_signals.meaning: player health degradation, potential removal from play

danger_signals.visual_form: fumble animation trigger or interception pass trajectory
danger_signals.meaning: loss of possession and field position

safety_signals.visual_form: receiver sprite separation from nearest defender, highlighting or color distinction
safety_signals.meaning: safe passing target with completion probability

safety_signals.visual_form: gap in offensive line formation, defender spacing
safety_signals.meaning: viable rushing direction with reduced contact risk

safety_signals.visual_form: game clock visual display and quarter indicator
safety_signals.meaning: time remaining for play execution and strategy adjustment

faction_indicators.color_identity: primary jersey color (varies by team selection)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: team logo displayed on scoreboard and field sideline
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: away team uses contrasting jersey color (typically white/light)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: home team bench positioned at consistent field location, crowd audio cues favor home team

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: away uniforms use lighter palette to contrast home team
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: away team bench positioned opposite home bench, crowd audio volume remains neutral or reduced

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: whistle animation and flag indicator sprites
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: referee position on field center, flag throws for penalty visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard first-down marker resets, chain placement resets, visual emphasis on new line to gain
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play clock resets, offensive play selection menu reopens, momentum audio cue

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: scoreboard and field grid indicate kicking distance, field position markers highlight proximity to goal line
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play calling shifts to field goal attempt options, kicker enters field of play

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: field color or grid pattern change in end zone area, scoreboard highlights goal proximity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play difficulty scaling increases, defense AI aggression heightens, turnover risk visualization

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: game clock visual emphasis, scoreboard urgency highlighting, field animation tempo acceleration
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play clock management becomes critical, timeout counter becomes visible UI element

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: final scoreboard display, player celebration or defeat animation sequence, crowd reaction visual
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: field clears, trophy or defeat sequence triggers, transition to post-game menu

environmental_storytelling.example: crowd roars louder and longer for home team touchdowns; reduced or absent for away team scoring
score differential visualization tells story of game momentum; visual dominance of leading team's color in display
yard line markers and field grid communicate strategic urgency; proximity to goal line affects play calling availability
sprite animation speed reduction and stance changes after high-intensity plays communicate exhaustion state
field texture and player movement animations reflect rain/mud conditions affecting gameplay difficulty

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: faster animation cycles, erratic throwing motion, increased fumble animation triggers
reduced sprite movement speed, limp animation, frequent ground contact without advancing
aggressive forward charging animation, increased collision detection triggers, gap filling speed
isolated sprite positioning away from defenders, animated hand-raising for target priority
animated approach and foot plant motion, ball trajectory arc visualization, success/miss animation completion

notes: Joe Montana II communicates game state primarily through scoreboard UI elements, field grid positioning, and sprite animation variations rather than complex environmental or faction-based visual systems typical of narrative-driven games. The paradigm is sports competition without political/factional conflict, so danger/safety signals relate to gameplay mechanics (defensive pressure, injury risk) rather than world-state threats. Visual manifestation of team identity relies on standard sports UI (jersey color, logo display, bench positioning) rather than symbolic or environmental storytelling. The game uses audio-visual crowd reactions as primary narrative device for momentum and dramatic tension.
